top.title: Un 49% de estadounidenses ya usan la IA con frecuencia. Solo un 16% cree que trae algo bueno
top.summary: Es un descubrimiento llamativo que revela un problema real: las conversaciones que mantenemos con la IA no son privadas, porque al preguntar algo a ChatGPT, Claude o Gemini, por ejemplo, los datos son enviados a las nubes de OpenAI, Anthropic o Google (respectivamente) para ser leídos y procesados. El 49% de los adultos estadounidenses afirman utilizar chatbots como ChatGPT, Gemini o Copilot, cuando ese porcentaje era del 33% en 2024 . top.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
top.summary: The prominent newspaper alleged that ChatGPT was illegally trained on its articles, infringed on its copyrights by outputting articles verbatim, and caused market harms by positioning ChatGPT as a substitute for a NYT subscription, as well as reputational harms by falsely attributing claims to NYT reporting. Model outputs show market harms, NYT alleged For the NYT, outputs shared during discovery—including a huge chunk of users’ ChatGPT sessions —remain some of the strongest evidence that OpenAI and Microsoft built tools that allegedly replaced the NYT by producing near-verbatim excerpts of its copyrighted works. In this case, OpenAI has argued that “ChatGPT is not a substitute for a Times s

Herramientas.title: The emergence of the web data infrastructure layer for AI
Herramientas.summary: In practice, many enterprise systems combine public web retrieval with APIs, licensed datasets, and proprietary internal data in their AI applications. The web was not designed for the automated discovery and retrieval that new AI applications demand. Herramientas.title: Repositioning retail for the AI era
Herramientas.summary: That momentum is now extending into conversational commerce through tools like Ask Macy’s, an AI-powered shopping assistant designed to act more like a personal stylist than a traditional search bar. By MIT Technology Review Insights archive page June 25, 2026 In partnership with Infosys Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping retail, but not in the ways consumers might immediately notice. As legacy retailers navigate a fragmented and hyper-competitive landscape, AI is becoming an operating philosophy.

What Europe’s heat wave means for the power grid
Regulación.summary: By Casey Crownhart archive page June 25, 2026 Pablo Blazquez Dominguez/Getty It’s been hard to look away from headlines about the European heat wave this week. Temperatures are breaking records across the continent, and the weather is threatening lives , shutting down schools , and in one particularly ironic case, forcing the cancellation of a London Climate Action Week event about extreme heat . As the summer ramps up and we see this kind of weather sweep around the Northern Hemisphere, I’m always keeping my eye on the power grid.

Investigación.title: IBM has unveiled chip technology that could help extend Moore’s Law another decade
Investigación.summary: By Sophia Chen archive page June 25, 2026 IBM's 0.7 nanometer prototype chip has transistors built in two layers to boost density. Photo via IBM EXECUTIVE SUMMARY IBM has built a new prototype chip with around 100 billion transistors on an area the size of a fingernail, which is twice the density of the company’s previous state-of-the-art technology announced in 2021. The design could pave the way for faster and more energy efficient computers for years to come.

Seguridad.summary: By Antonio Regalado archive page June 24, 2026 Photo illustration by Sarah Rogers/MITTR | Photos Shutterstock EXECUTIVE SUMMARY The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now the payment company Stripe, founded by brothers Patrick and John Collison, says it will fund a new $500 million nonprofit whose goal is preventing both the common cold and the flu.
